children.name: Notiomystis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Stitchbird, a unique New Zealand passerine that was long thought to be a honeyeater but is now placed in its own family. It has a brush-tipped tongue for nectar feeding and shows remarkable sexual dimorphism, with the male having a striking black-and-yellow head pattern. It is highly social and often visits feeding stations in sanctuaries.

children.name: Notiomystis cincta
children.description: A striking, sexually dimorphic bird with the male bearing a black head, white ear-tufts, and a brilliant yellow band across the chest, endemic to New Zealand. It feeds on nectar, fruit, and invertebrates, using its brush-tipped tongue to extract nectar from flax and native flowers. Once widespread, it now survives only on offshore islands and in predator-proof sanctuaries, where it is a flagship species for New Zealand conservation.
